questions.explanation: Edge processing reduces round-trip time to the cloud and can filter/aggregate data locally, lowering bandwidth costs and improving responsiveness.

options.A: To increase latency for better batching efficiency
options.B: To reduce latency and bandwidth usage for time-sensitive workloads
options.C: To eliminate the need for device authentication
options.D: To avoid using containers and orchestration

questions.question: In edge computing architecture, what is the primary reason to process data at or near the data source (the edge) instead of only in a central cloud region?

questions.explanation: Distributed systems are designed to tolerate partial failures; nodes and networks can fail independently while the system aims to remain available/correct.

options.A: All components share a single memory space
options.B: The system continues operating correctly even when some nodes fail
options.C: Network communication is instantaneous and reliable
options.D: There is always exactly one machine in the system

questions.question: Which statement best describes a key property of distributed systems?

questions.explanation: top provides an interactive, real-time view of processes and system resource utilization.
questions.question: Which Linux command is commonly used to view real-time system and process resource usage (CPU/memory) interactively?

questions.explanation: FROM sets the base image (and optionally a build stage) that the rest of the Dockerfile builds upon.
questions.question: Which Dockerfile instruction is primarily used to specify the base image for a container build?

questions.explanation: K3s and MicroK8s are designed to run Kubernetes with a smaller footprint, making them suitable for edge and resource-constrained environments.

options.D: A full OpenStack control plane on the device

questions.question: You need to deploy a lightweight Kubernetes cluster on an edge device with limited CPU/RAM. Which option is most appropriate?

questions.explanation: MQTT is a lightweight pub/sub protocol designed for constrained devices and networks, using a broker-based model.
questions.question: Which IoT protocol is best known for a lightweight publish/subscribe messaging model commonly used over unreliable networks?

questions.explanation: OpenTelemetry provides standardized APIs/SDKs and collectors for distributed tracing and context propagation across services.

options.A: Use OpenTelemetry SDKs/collectors to emit traces and propagate context between services
options.B: Only scrape node CPU metrics with Prometheus
options.C: Enable Kubernetes NetworkPolicy and read dropped packet counters
options.D: Rely exclusively on container stdout logs without correlation IDs

questions.question: You want end-to-end request tracing across microservices running in Kubernetes, with vendor-neutral instrumentation. Which approach best fits?

questions.explanation: Unique device certificates enable strong identity, revocation, and rotation; mTLS provides mutual authentication and encryption consistent with Zero Trust principles.

options.A: Use a shared pre-shared key (PSK) embedded in all devices
options.B: Issue each device a unique X.509 certificate from a PKI and enforce mTLS with certificate rotation
options.C: Disable certificate validation to avoid clock-skew issues
options.D: Use HTTP without TLS inside the network because it is 'trusted'

questions.question: An edge gateway must authenticate devices and encrypt traffic using mutual TLS. Which design best supports scalable device identity and rotation?
questions.topic: Security (Pki, Tls, Device Identity, Zero Trust)

questions.explanation: Tracing and latency metrics pinpoint whether delays occur in the service, network, or database; correlating with Kubernetes events/logs provides evidence-driven diagnosis.

options.A: Increase the replica count and ignore the restarts
options.B: Add distributed tracing and latency metrics (e.g., OpenTelemetry + Prometheus) to identify where time is spent, then correlate with pod events/logs
options.C: Disable readiness probes so Kubernetes stops restarting the pod
options.D: Rebuild the container image with a different base OS without collecting evidence

questions.question: A microservice deployed on Kubernetes is intermittently failing readiness checks and being restarted. You suspect a dependency (database) is slow. What is the most effective first step to debug this in a production-like environment?

questions.explanation: Durable local buffering with asynchronous forwarding matches intermittent networks and reduces coupling; at-least-once delivery improves reliability under disconnections.

options.A: Use synchronous request/response calls from devices directly to a cloud API with no local buffering
options.B: Use a local message queue/broker with durable storage and at-least-once delivery, then forward asynchronously to the cloud when connectivity is available
options.C: Store all telemetry only in device RAM to maximize performance
options.D: Require a single global database transaction spanning edge and cloud components

questions.question: You are designing an edge-to-cloud ingestion pipeline. Edge devices send telemetry to a local broker, which forwards to a cloud edge service (AWS/Azure/GCP). The system must tolerate intermittent connectivity and avoid data loss. Which architecture choice best aligns software behavior with the underlying system architecture constraints?

topics_covered: Security (Pki, Tls, Device Identity, Zero Trust)
